title: Same-lap field context features
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions: undercuts, overcuts, traffic, and mass pit timing depend on where a car sits versus the field, not only its own tyre age.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions: undercuts, overcuts, traffic, and mass pit timing depend on where a car sits versus the field, not only its own tyre age. Current best features use race/compound medians over broad groups, but not same-race same-lap percentiles or field composition.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year/Race + LapNumber, add leak-safe cross-sectional features: TyreLife percentile, Cumulative_Degradation percentile, LapTime_Delta z-score, Position percentile, count/share of cars on each Compound, count of PitStop==1 on the current lap, and whether this driver is an early/late outlier for its compound/stint.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking around pit waves and undercut windows, especially when many cars approach the same strategic lap together.
risk: If validation computes group statistics using held-out rows differently from test-time availability, CV may be optimistic; keep features strictly same-lap and target-free.
